request.prompt:
REVIEW-ONLY validation gate. Do NOT modify code. Reply AGREE or list concrete severity-tagged objections (critical/major/suggestion; critical/major MUST cite file:line + a concrete failure scenario, else it is a suggestion).

CONTEXT: Build feature robo-chat-model, task 001 raises the VS Code API floor. Locked requirement AC3 (requirements.md:20) requires engines.vscode >= ^1.122.0 (VS Code 1.122, May 2026, removed the Copilot sign-in requirement for extension-contributed models -> signed-out usage works). DECISIONS.md 'Engines / types floor (criterion 3)' ADDITIONALLY pinned @types/vscode to ^1.122.0.

PROBLEM: @types/vscode ^1.122.0 is NOT published on npm. Highest published @types/vscode is 1.120.0 (npm view @types/vscode version => 1.120.0). 'npm install' fails ETARGET. The LanguageModelChatProvider API (registerLanguageModelChatProvider, provideLanguageModelChatInformation/Response, toolCalling capability) was FINALIZED in VS Code 1.104 (research note 003, corroborated). The 1.122 change was runtime/licensing (no sign-in), NOT a new typings symbol.

PROPOSED RESOLUTION: Keep engines.vscode ^1.122.0 (unchanged; satisfies AC3 runtime floor + engines assertion). Decouple the typings pin: set @types/vscode to ^1.120.0 (highest published; already contains the full finalized LM-chat-provider API surface since 1.104). Update package-lock accordingly so npm install/compile succeed. Update the colocated manifest test to assert engines.vscode ^1.122 (unchanged) but NOT require @types/vscode ^1.122. Record the change in DECISIONS.md as a forced decoupling (typings lag the engine floor; standard practice).

QUESTION: Does decoupling @types/vscode (^1.120.0) from engines.vscode (^1.122.0) still satisfy AC3 and let task 001 compile, while preserving the substantive signed-out requirement? Or does compiling the provider (task 006) against 1.120 typings risk missing any API symbol that only exists in 1.122 typings? Focus dimensions: correctness, api-contract, test-adequacy.
